summary: No false-pass/tautology. 5/5 source mutations flip the expected test RED (both oracle un-skips, disjointness boundary, weather ceiling, effective-deadline max). 2 WARN: stale min/earlier + Skipped comments.

findings.claim: computeEffectiveDeadline both-set returns the later (max) value
findings.evidence: Assertions are CORRECT (MUTATION max->min flips tests 1-3 RED) but header line 3 says 'the earlier of the two non-null values' and test name line 63 says 'min of equal = either' — stale min-era prose contradicts the asserted max; a maintainer could invert the assertion to match the comment and reintroduce the R50.0 regression SPEC AC-840-4 corrected.
findings.fix: Update header line 3 and test name line 63 to MAX/later wording, aligned to SPEC AC-840-4 determination.

findings.claim: CASE-1a-preferred and CASE-10a are the leg's active regression oracles
findings.evidence: Lines 177 and 591 still read 'Skipped so main stays green; un-skip when the follow-up lands' directly above the now-un-skipped, GREEN-running oracle tests — misleading, implies inert tests that are in fact the primary pre-fix RED reproduction.
findings.fix: Remove the stale 'Skipped ...' comment lines above the active oracle tests.

findings.evidence: Suite covers only 2-entry days; the impl compares adjacent sorted pairs only (runSchedule.js:260-264), so a 3+-entry day where a long early task overlaps a non-adjacent later task is unpinned. Not a false-pass in existing tests — an edge-completeness gap.
findings.fix: Add a 3-entry-per-day overlap case to telly's suite.

findings.evidence: Row still reads min() and 'pending (bert authors)' while SPEC AC-840-4 DETERMINATION corrected it to max() and effective-deadline.test.js now exists and is GREEN.
findings.fix: Kermit: update AC-840-4 traceability row to max() and the real test path.

refers.why: Stale min/earlier + Skipped comments and a 3-entry disjointness edge gap in telly-owned test files
